items.title: In-memory dictionary
items.summary: Let's begin our journey to understand persistent memory by building an in-memory dictionary.

items.title: Persistent memory - Introduction
items.summary: Do you want to learn about persistent memory? Join this journey to explore persistent memory and build a persistent dictionary.

items.title: How To Use switch In Modern Java
items.summary: Since Java 14 introduced switch expressions, using switch isn't as straight-forward as it used to be: colons or arrows, statement or expression, labels or patterns? Here's how to best use switch in modern Java.

items.title: Funny programming comics
items.summary: A collection of funny comics for programmers.

items.title: Virtual Thread Deep Dive - Inside Java Newscast #23
items.summary: Now that Project Loom's JEP 425 officially proposes virtual threads, it's time to take a close look at them: scheduling and memory management; mounting, unmounting, capturing, and pinning; observability; and and what you can do for optimal scalability - this episode has (almost) everything on virtual threads!

items.title: Kotlin Coroutines: Deep Dive book is finally ready!
items.summary: Learn about our recently published book dedicated to Kotlin Coroutines.

items.title: All About JDK 18 - Inside Java Newscast #21
items.summary: Refinements in pattern matching and Panama's foreign and vector APIs; a new command jwebserver and a new IP address resolution SPI; preparing code for UTF-8 becoming the default character set and for the eventual removal of finalization; and a few more bits and pieces.

items.title: SharedFlow and StateFlow
items.summary: Known as a replacement for Subject, LiveData, and many more. SharedFlow and StateFlow are powerful coroutines classes, every Kotlin developer should know.

items.title: Modern Java - Ask Me Anything
items.summary: New language features, API additions, and JVM improvements; projects Amber, Loom, and Panama, Valhalla, Leyden, and Babylon; shorter release cadence and free Oracle JDK - there's a lot going on in modern Java. I'll do my best to answer all your questions about it.

items.title: State of Pattern Matching with Brian Goetz
items.summary: Conversation with Project Amber lead Brian Goetz about pattern matching in Java: Why Map::get should be a pattern, the linear thinking trap and how it impacts null handling in pattern matched, exhaustiveness in switch statements and the rehabilitation of switch .

items.title: Configuring Maven For Compiled And Tested Code In Javadoc
items.summary: For JDK 18's / JEP 413's embedded snippets to be compiled and tested by your Maven build, they need to be added to a source set, Surefire needs to pick them up, and Javadoc needs to know their location - here's how to do that.

items.title: Compiled And Tested Code In Javadoc - Inside Java Newscast #20
items.summary: Short code snippets in Javadoc are a great way to document an API, but they're brittle. JDK 18 / JEP 413 solves that problem by allowing us to reference snippets from external files that are compiled and tested.

items.title: Funny programming videos
items.summary: A collection of funny videos for programmers.

items.title: More Opinions On Optional
items.summary: How much work is it to wrap Optional ? Do you need to null -check Optional arguments? What about serializability and framework support? And why consider the type in the first place? Answers in here!

items.title: Where to use Optional - Inside Java Newscast #19
items.summary: Is it ok to use Optional as parameter type? Overloading is an alternative, but how well does it stack up? Thoughts on this question (and other Optional -related ones) can usually be put into one of three (and a half) categories.

items.title: Java's Plans for 2022 - Inside Java Newscast #18
items.summary: An update on Java's four key projects: Valhalla, Panama, Loom, and Amber - what they're about, where they are right now, and what their plans are for 2022 and beyond.

items.title: Advent of Kotlin Solutions
items.summary: Solutions to the Advent of Kotlin!

items.title: Advent of Kotlin: Week 4
items.summary: Week 4 of the Advent of Kotlin: Parsing JSON

items.title: Project Loom Brings Structured Concurrency - Inside Java Newscast #17
items.summary: Project Loom aims to bring structured concurrency to Java, a concept that compares to "regular" concurrency like structured programming compares to GOTO-programming - let's dip our toes into this new concept. Also: JDK 18 feature freeze, JDK migration guide, and nifty things to do with the new simple web server.

items.title: Advent of Kotlin: Week 3
items.summary: Week 3 of the Advent of Kotlin: k-means clustering

items.title: Advent of Kotlin: Week 2
items.summary: Week 2 of the Advent of Kotlin: Tree algorithms

items.title: Advent of Kotlin: Week 1: JSON stringify and possible value parentheses
items.summary: Week 1 of the Advent of Kotlin: JSON stringify and possible well-formed parentheses

items.title: Variables point to objects
items.summary: A basic feature, that is commonly misunderstood.

items.title: What Happens to Finalization in JDK 18? - Inside Java Newscast #15
items.summary: Finalization was part of Java from day one to help developers manage resources but it turns out that it's really not good at that. Here's why and what's gonna happen next. Also, reflection and method handles.

items.title: Java Next
items.summary: From Amber to Valhalla, from Loom to Leyden, from Babylon to Panama - six big projects are shaping Java's future and while some of them are already crossing the finishing line, others are just getting started. Let's take a closer look at how they will improve Java.

items.title: 11 Tricks From dev.java - Inside Java Newscast #14
items.summary: From compact record constructors to boolean expressions in pattern matching, from generic wildcards to chaining predicates and comparators, from jpackage to jlink - here are 11 Java tricks handpicked from dev.java.

items.title: Testing Kotlin Coroutines
items.summary: How we test Kotlin Coroutines, including common Android and backend cases.

items.title: Constructing a coroutine scope
items.summary: How we generally define coroutine scope on Android and on the backend.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 56: Consider using groupingBy instead of groupBy
items.summary: What Grouping is, and how groupingBy instead of groupBy can be a performance optimization.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 55: Consider associating elements to a map
items.summary: How associateBy is useful to improve the performance of finding elements.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 54: Prefer Sequences for big collections with more than one processing step
items.summary: What the difference between list and sequence processing is, and when each should be preferred.

items.title: Faster LTS and free JDK with Java 17 - Inside Java Newscast #12
items.summary: Java 17 comes with more than just new features. A faster LTS cadence and free Oracle JDK make this the best-supported modern release ever.

items.title: Better Random Number Generation in Java 17
items.summary: Java 17 expands the API for random number generation to make it more usable, extensible, and robust with RandomGenerator and RandomGeneratorFactory at its core.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 53: Eliminate obsolete object references
items.summary: How to help our garbage collector and avoid memory leaks.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 52: Consider using inline value classes
items.summary: What value classes are, how to use and inline them.

items.title: Coroutines under the hood
items.summary: A deep dive into how suspension and continuations work under the hood.

items.title: The State of Project Valhalla with Brian Goetz
items.summary: Conversation with Project Valhalla lead Brian Goetz about Java's original sin, unifying the type system, expanding generics, current work, the project timeline, and more.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 51: Use the inline modifier for functions with parameters of functional types
items.summary: How inline functions work and why they can be so important for the performance of our application.

items.title: Fast and Secure Inter-process Communication on JDK 16 - Inside Java Newscast #11
items.summary: JDK 16's socket channel / server-socket channel API can use Unix domain sockets for faster and more secure inter-process communication on the same host - also: JDK 17 final release candidate and Oracle Developer Live

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 47: Avoid unnecessary object creation
items.summary: About the most essential rule of performance optimization.

items.title: Traits for testing in Kotlin
items.summary: A trick needed when using traits for testing in Kotlin.

items.title: Random Numbers and JDK Flight Recorder - Inside Java Newscast #9
items.summary: The new API for random number generation in Java 17 - why it needed to change and how the new API is more usable, extensible, and robust - and how to get started with JDK Flight Recorder, particularly on Java 16.

items.title: How does suspension work in Kotlin coroutines?
items.summary: A deep explanation of how suspension works in Kotlin Coroutines.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 32: Consider factory functions instead of secondary constructors
items.summary: What factory functions are and why they are so important patterns for object creation.

items.title: Effective Kotlin Item 46: Avoid member extensions
items.summary: What member extensions are, how they are possible and why we should avoid using them.

items.title: Incremental Evolution, Pattern Switches vs Visitor Pattern, and Wayland Support - Inside Java Newscast #8
items.summary: How the six-month release cadence enabled a more incremental evolution of the Java platform and how pattern switches and sealed classes are an alternative to the visitor pattern. Also, maybe Wayland support for Java.

items.title: Visitor Pattern Considered Pointless - Use Pattern Switches Instead
items.summary: In modern Java, the visitor pattern is no longer needed. Using sealed types and switches with pattern matching achieves the same goals with less code and less complexity.

items.title: Handling null and Upgrading Past Java 8 - Inside Java Newscast #7
items.summary: Dealing with null is never fun, so in this newscast, I explain how to best handle it, what tools can do, and how recent and upcoming language changes help dealing with it. I'll also look at a recent blog post about how Netflix upgraded from Java 8 to 16.

items.title: The State of Project Panama with Maurizio Cimadamore
items.summary: Conversation with Project Panama lead Maurizio Cimadamore about the project's core mission, the split into foreign-memory access and foreign linker APIs, jextract, performance, interaction with Project Valhalla, the timeline, and more.

items.title: Java Modules in Real Life
items.summary: Advice on why, when, when not, and how to use Java modules in real life for your projects

items.title: kt.academy new logo: Phoenix
items.summary: Why we decided to change our logo, and what is the symbolism of Phoenix.

items.title: The State of Project Loom with Ron Pressler
items.summary: Conversation with Project Loom lead Ron Pressler about the project's core mission, challenges like interaction with debuggers and garbage collectors, the timeline for the next steps, compatibility and more.

items.title: Java 17: Features and Development - Inside Java Newscast #6
items.summary: Java 17, the next long-term support release, enters feature freeze and the release preparations begin today (June 10th). A good time to take a closer look at the list of JEPs as well as the development process.

items.title: Pattern Matching in Switches and Security Manager Deprecation - Inside Java Newscast #5
items.summary: JEP 406, which is a candidate for Java 17, introduced pattern matching in switch statements and expressions, introduces guarded patterns, and improves null handling. Then we need to talk about JEP 411's deprecation of the security manager.

items.title: 26 Hours of Java
items.summary: On May 29th we'll throw a late birthday party for Java, which turned 26 a few days before. With a 26-hour live stream relay race! 🥳

items.title: Eve Marder on the Crucial Resilience of Neurons
items.summary: Eve Marder’s research into the plasticity and resilience of nervous systems finds universal principles guiding life’s responses to stress. The post Eve Marder on the Crucial Resilience of Neurons first appeared on Quanta Magazine

items.title: 5 Secret Java API Tricks and Record Semantics - Inside Java Newscast #4
items.summary: Five nifty Java API features that you need to know (and many more in the linked thread) and a quick explanation why Java records are not about reducing boilerplate.

items.title: Charlie Marcus Knows That Quantum Facts Aren’t Complicated
items.summary: The secret to making a qubit for future quantum computers might depend on knowing how to tie knots in unusual materials, argues the physicist Charlie Marcus. The post Charlie Marcus Knows That Quantum Facts Aren’t Complicated first appeared on Quanta Magazine

items.title: Why Java's Records Are Better* Than Lombok's @Data and Kotlin's Data Classes
items.summary: While all three remove boilerplate, the similarities don't go much further. Records have stronger semantics with important downstream benefits, which makes them better*. (* not always; depends on circumstances; excuse the clickbait)

items.title: Amie Wilkinson Sees the Dynamic Chaos in Puff Pastry
items.summary: To a dynamicist like Amie Wilkinson, understanding the universe is about knowing all the right moves. The post Amie Wilkinson Sees the Dynamic Chaos in Puff Pastry first appeared on Quanta Magazine
